title: echium sabulicola subsp. sabulicola
intro: Echium sabulicola subsp. sabulicola is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae), commonly known as a plant with medicinal properties. It is native to the Macaronesia region of the Atlantic coast and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Echium sabulicola subsp. sabulicola can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and wounds.
